title: AI Strategy for Executives: A 90-Day Operating Plan
excerpt: Most AI initiatives fail because they start with tools. This 90-day operating plan starts with workflows, measurable outcomes, and leadership discipline.
contentHtml:
Most AI strategies fail for one predictable reason: they start with tools, not workflows.

When leadership teams anchor on vendor demos before operating priorities, they create fragmented pilots with no durable business impact.

A better approach: 90 days, three phases

Days 1-30: Choose the right workflows

  • Map your top 10 workflows by cost, delay, and customer impact.
  • Select 2-3 high-friction workflows where AI can materially reduce cycle time.
  • Define baseline metrics before implementation.

At this stage, success is not adoption. Success is precision in scope.

Days 31-60: Run controlled execution

  • Ship one production use case end-to-end, not five disconnected pilots.
  • Assign single-threaded ownership for delivery and outcomes.
  • Track operational metrics weekly: cycle time, quality, exception rate, and business impact.

This phase is where execution discipline separates signal from noise.

Days 61-90: Scale what works

  • Standardize architecture and governance for proven use cases.
  • Train managers on workflow redesign, not just prompting behavior.
  • Expand only where measured impact is repeatable.

Scale is earned through evidence, not enthusiasm.

Leadership mistakes to avoid

  • Treating AI as an innovation theater activity
  • Delegating ownership too far down the org
  • Measuring activity instead of outcomes
  • Ignoring change management in frontline teams

Executive scorecard

For each initiative, ask four questions:

  1. What workflow are we improving?
  2. Which KPI should move in 90 days?
  3. Who owns the outcome?
  4. What is the decision if we do not see impact?

AI is not a side project. It is an operating model upgrade. Leadership teams that execute with rigor will build advantage. Everyone else will collect experiments.
